stories.title: Knicks Parade 2 Days Away — Manhattan Prepares for Canyon of Heroes
stories.angle: The Knicks' championship ticker-tape parade is Thursday, June 18 — two days away. Multiple outlets are calling it one of the biggest anticipated parades ever in NYC. The route: Battery Park → north along Broadway through the Canyon of Heroes → City Hall Plaza, where Mayor Mamdani will host a Key to the City ceremony. What makes this extraordinary: the Knicks won championships in 1970 and 1973 but NEVER received an official Canyon of Heroes ticker-tape parade. Thursday is the first in franchise history.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Two days. That's it.

Thursday, June 18 — 10:00 AM.
Battery Park → Canyon of Heroes → City Hall Plaza.

The Knicks won in 1970. Won in 1973.
They NEVER got a Canyon of Heroes parade. Until now.

Thursday is the first in franchise history. 🏆🗽 #NewYorkKnicks

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Quick Knicks parade checklist:

📅 Thursday, June 18
⏰ 10:00 AM
📍 Battery Park to City Hall
🏛️ Key to the City ceremony after
🔵🟠 City Hall goes blue & orange

Plan your spot in the Canyon of Heroes. #NewYorkKnicks

article.headline: Knicks Parade Thursday: The First Canyon of Heroes Celebration in Franchise History

research.facts:
Knicks Parade — June 18 Canyon of Heroes
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Parade: Thursday, June 18, 2026 — 10:00 AM start
- Route: Battery Park → Broadway → Canyon of Heroes → City Hall Plaza
- Mayor Zohran Mamdani hosting Key to the City ceremony at City Hall Plaza
- City Hall and municipal buildings to be illuminated blue and orange
- Expected to be one of the biggest parades in NYC history per multiple outlets
- First official Canyon of Heroes ticker-tape parade in Knicks franchise history (won in 1970 and 1973 but never received a Canyon of Heroes parade)
research.sources:
- Sources: Fox5NY, Yahoo Sports, Sports Illustrated, Timeout NY
**Key context:**
- Canyon of Heroes route is ~1 mile through Lower Manhattan
- Same path used by other championship teams, astronauts, world leaders historically

stories.title: Giannis Trade War — Bucks Reject Heat Package, Celtics Push Jaylen Brown
stories.angle: The Giannis trade market is heating up with 7 days to the June 23 deadline. New developments: The Bucks "do not love" what the Heat are offering (Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jacquez Jr. + No. 13 pick) and are actively looking for other options. The Celtics have made a formal offer centered on Jaylen Brown — but that comes with its own stumbling block: breaking up the Tatum/Brown partnership. Meanwhile, insider Marc Stein reports a deal is "indeed drawing near." Giannis reportedly wants Miami behind the scenes.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
GIANNIS UPDATE 🚨

Bucks "do not love" what Miami is offering.
Heat package: Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jacquez Jr. + No. 13 pick.

Bucks are now looking for better options.
Celtics offer: Jaylen Brown + picks.

7 days to the June 23 deadline. This market is MOVING. #MilwaukeeBucks

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The Giannis trade war is on.

Marc Stein: deal "indeed drawing near."

🔥 Bucks rejected Heat package
🍀 Celtics push Jaylen Brown
💚 Giannis wants Miami behind the scenes

Clock hits zero June 23. Seven days left. #GiannisAntetokounmpo

research.facts:
Giannis Trade — Bucks Reject Heat, Celtics Emerging
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Marc Stein reports deal "indeed drawing near" before June 23 NBA Draft
- Bucks co-owner Jimmy Haslam wants deal finalized before June 23
- Giannis wants Miami behind the scenes per multiple insiders
- Heat package: Tyler Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jaime Jacquez Jr. + No. 13 pick (2026 draft)
- Bucks "do not love what the Heat are offering" — actively seeking other options
- Celtics have made formal offer centered on Jaylen Brown
- Celtics offer stumbling block: would break up Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown
- Heat and Celtics considered two frontrunners per NBC Sports
- Knicks effectively out of Giannis discussions after winning title
**Sources:** CBS Sports, NBC Sports, Bleacher Report x2, Boston.com

stories.title: Dybantsa Pre-Draft Visits — Meetings With Wizards and Jazz "Went Well"
stories.angle: AJ Dybantsa completed pre-draft visits with the Washington Wizards (Friday, June 13) and the Utah Jazz (Saturday, June 14). His father Ace Dybantsa told reporters both meetings "went well." Darryn Peterson also visited the Wizards. The draft is June 23-24 at Barclays Center Brooklyn — 7 days away. Dybantsa remains the heavy favorite at -450 to go No. 1 overall to Washington.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
AJ Dybantsa visited the Wizards on Friday. Visited the Jazz on Saturday.

His dad: both meetings "went well."

Still the heavy favorite at -450 to go No. 1 to Washington.

7 days until the 2026 NBA Draft. Barclays Center. June 23. 🏀

stories.title: Austin Reaves — Lakers' Exclusive Window Closes June 30, Nets Lurking
stories.angle: The Lakers hold an exclusive negotiating window with Austin Reaves that closes when free agency opens June 30. If they don't get a deal done in that window, Reaves hits the open market and the Brooklyn Nets — who are expected to offer a 4-year, $178.5M max — will be waiting. The Lakers can counter with a 5-year, $239M deal. Reaves (23.3 PPG / 5.5 APG / 4.7 RPG, age 28) has leverage and Laker sources say he wants to stay, but the Nets have already signaled they'll push hard.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Lakers have until June 30 to lock up Austin Reaves before the Nets pounce.

Nets offer: 4-year, $178.5M
Lakers can offer: 5-year, $239M

Reaves wants to stay in LA. But this window closes FAST.

23.3 PPG. 5.5 APG. Age 28. The Lakers cannot afford to lose him. #LakersNation

article.headline: Austin Reaves: Lakers Hold Exclusive Window but Nets Are Waiting With Max Offer

- Lakers can offer 5-year, $239M deal (MEDIUM — reported widely)

- Atlanta Hawks and Detroit Pistons also interested

stories.title: Knicks Dynasty Building — How New York Defends the Crown
stories.angle: With the parade on Thursday and the offseason officially beginning, attention turns to how the Knicks defend the 2026 title. Key decisions: Karl-Anthony Towns' extension timeline, OG Anunoby re-signing priority, and whether GM Scott Perry targets another piece at the trade deadline. Brunson's team-friendly contract ($156.5M through 2028) remains the foundation. The Knicks will enter 2026-27 as defending champions and the clear favorite in the East.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The parade is Thursday. Then what?

Knicks enter the offseason as defending champs. The questions:

• KAT extension?
• OG Anunoby re-sign?
• Trade at the deadline?

Brunson locked in at $156.5M through 2028. Foundation is solid.

East better watch out. #NewYorkKnicks

article.headline: Dynasty Mode: How the Knicks Plan to Defend Their NBA Championship

research.facts:
Knicks Offseason / Dynasty Analysis
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Brunson contract: 4-year, $156.5M extension (team-friendly, runs through 2028)
- Knicks won 2026 NBA Finals over Spurs 4-1
- First Knicks title since 1973 (53-year drought)
- Key offseason decisions: Karl-Anthony Towns extension, OG Anunoby re-signing
- Knicks will be defending champions entering 2026-27
**Analysis/Context (appropriately hedged):**
- Knicks have salary cap flexibility due to Brunson's team-friendly deal
- Will be Eastern Conference favorites as defending champs
